campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the city's finances for July 2025, including how the city's investments performed and overall money management. The council also discussed plans for improving transportation options around Campbell, looking at ways to make it easier for residents to get around using different methods of travel.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment performance and financial transactions
City treasurer's monthly financial report
Campbell Multimodal Transportation Plan - improving transit, biking, and walking options

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 where residents had a chance to speak about a proposed new rule or regulation. After hearing from the public, the council introduced an ordinance (a new city law) and decided to skip the normal first reading process so they could vote on adopting it at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident input on proposed ordinance
First reading waived to expedite the ordinance adoption process
Ordinance scheduled for final vote at next regular council meeting
Fast-track approval process used instead of standard multi-meeting procedure

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — March 17, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on March 17, 2026, to approve routine meeting minutes, review the city's finances and budget from January, and check progress on the General Plan and housing goals. The council also approved a new residential development project (Solana Project) and finalized plans for community health and senior services in 2026.
keyTopics: Approval of Solana Project residential development (Tract 10657)
January 2026 financial and investment report review
Progress update on General Plan and Housing Element goals
2026 Community Health and Senior Services work plan
City council procedural updates to meeting minutes format

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 1, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a planning meeting to organize upcoming council sessions scheduled for April 7 and April 14, 2026. The council reviewed which items to add to their agendas, decided on timing for discussions, and checked legal notice requirements for any waivered documents.
keyTopics: April 7, 2026 final agenda preparation and new item additions
April 14, 2026 draft agenda review
Setting specific time slots for agenda items to manage meeting flow
Legal notice waiver requirements for council documents
Public record review from March 19-26, 2026

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 2, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council's Human Relations Committee meeting scheduled for April 2, 2026 has been canceled. Residents should mark their calendars for the next meeting, which will be held on May 7, 2026.
keyTopics: Human Relations Committee meeting canceled
Next meeting rescheduled to May 7, 2026
No specific agenda items discussed

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Housing and Human Services Commission met to review applications for federal grants that help fund housing and community development projects in the city. The group approved meeting notes from their previous month and heard presentations from organizations seeking this grant money to support local housing and services.
keyTopics: Federal housing grant applications being reviewed (CDBG funding)
Community development projects seeking city funding support
Approval of previous meeting minutes from February

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 6, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a meeting on April 6, 2026, where residents had the opportunity to speak about any city matters for up to 3 minutes each. The main business included selecting a new chair and vice chair for the Legislative Review Committee for 2026, along with reviewing the city's legislative calendar for the year.
keyTopics: Public comment period - residents can address the council on any jurisdiction matter
Election of Legislative Review Committee leadership for 2026
2026 legislative calendar and priorities
State law limits council decisions to items officially on the meeting agenda

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 1, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara's Audit Committee held a meeting to review a report about how Silicon Valley Power handles its power purchase agreements. The city is offering both in-person and online attendance options so residents can participate however is most convenient for them.
keyTopics: Review of Silicon Valley Power's power purchase agreement process
Hybrid meeting format (in-person and Zoom options available)
City Auditor's Office report findings
Public participation options via phone or video

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — March 17, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met on March 17, 2026 to discuss a new program offering home security camera grants to residents, review the city's spending plans for the next five years (2027-2031), and go over the city's finances for that same period. The council also approved previous meeting minutes and scheduled upcoming meetings.
keyTopics: Home Security Camera Grant Program - new initiative to help residents install cameras
Five-Year Capital Improvement Plan (2027-2031) - what city projects and infrastructure upgrades are planned
FY2027-2031 Budget Outlook - how the city plans to spend money over the next five years
Meeting minutes approved and calendar reviewed

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 31, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to review and approve the city's gas utility budget and rates for the next fiscal year (2027). The council considered changes to residential and commercial gas rates and discussed how utility reserves and funds will be managed.
keyTopics: Gas utility budget forecast for fiscal year 2027
Rate changes for residential gas customers (Schedule G-1)
Rate changes for commercial and large commercial gas service (Schedules G-2 and G-3)
Transfer of utility reserves and general fund money
